title: Iliad, Book 23: The Funeral Games
description: Book 23 of Homer's Iliad: The Funeral Games.
introduction: Book 23 of the Iliad: The Funeral Games. The Greek text is from the Venetus A manuscript (10th century), the oldest complete manuscript of the Iliad.

translation.text: The Greeks mourned Patroclus. Achilles held funeral games: chariot racing, boxing, wrestling, a footrace, armed combat, discus, archery, and javelin. The games were fierce but fair, and Achilles distributed prizes with generosity.
